growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (CDM) with glucose and lactic acid

up.method: Monod affinity constant K_S; no volumetric uptake rate reported
Primary electron acceptor (qualitative); no rate measured

growth_records.cite: Sangha JS et al. Advancing dental biofilm models: the integral role of pH in predicting S. mutans colonization. mSphere (2024). DOI:10.1128/msphere.00743-24. [PMC11774048]
growth_records.snippet: Neisseria subflava Glucose 0.261 5.55 1.00 ... N. subflava is an aerobic species that consumes glucose and has oxygen as the primary electron acceptor, producing acetate and formate
growth_records.notes: mu_max = 0.261 h-1 (Table 2), K_S = 5.55 mmol/L (=1.00 g/L glucose). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.261 = 2.656 h. Kinetic parameters CITED FROM Sangha et al. 2024, Microbiol Spectr, doi:10.1128/spectrum.03713-23 (ref 17). Products (acetate, formate) qualitative from cited literature. Modeling paper; no new measured rates.
